item.title: AACR 2026 Conference
item.description:
• Summary: At the AACR 2026 conference, key oncology data were presented, including updated results for Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) in smoldering multiple myeloma and strong efficacy for Revolution Medicines' KRAS inhibitor zoldonrasib. Insights into targets like CCR8 and VEGF highlighted advances in CAR-T and targeted therapies.
  • MoAs: Carvykti: BCMA-directed CAR-T cell therapy; zoldonrasib: KRAS G12C inhibitor
  • Sources: STAT News, BioPharma Dive, ApexOnco, ApexOnco, ApexOnco, STAT News

item.title: Revolution Medicines / KRAS Inhibitors
item.description:
• Summary: Revolution Medicines continues to advance its KRAS inhibitors, with daraxonrasib showing promise in pancreatic cancer and lung cancer. The development underscores progress in targeting previously undruggable KRAS mutations.
  • MoAs: Daraxonrasib: KRAS G12D inhibitor
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News, Endpoints News

item.title: Agenus Cancer Cocktail Trial
item.description:
• Summary: Agenus reported that its cancer cocktail trial recorded a 0% response rate, missing the primary endpoint, but the company is focusing on survival data for further analysis.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioSpace, Fierce Biotech

item.title: Novartis Pipeline Cuts
item.description:
• Summary: Novartis has discontinued two research programs aimed at cancer-related blood clots, as reported by Endpoints News. This pipeline refinement likely reflects strategic realignment or unfavorable clinical data in the thrombosis-oncology intersection.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Gilead / Arcus TIGIT Option Decline
item.description:
• Summary: Gilead has declined an option on a TIGIT inhibitor from Arcus following a phase 3 clinical trial failure in cancer immunotherapy. This decision reflects setbacks in the development of TIGIT-targeted therapies, which aim to enhance anti-tumor immune responses.
  • MoAs: TIGIT inhibitors, such as those from Arcus (e.g., domvanalimab), block the TIGIT receptor to prevent immune suppression and promote T-cell activity against tumors.
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Merck PD-1/VEGF Data
item.description:
• Summary: Merck has released early data on a combination of a PD-1 inhibitor (likely pembrolizumab) and a VEGF inhibitor for lung cancer, showing competitive efficacy. However, upcoming summits may present challenges as the landscape evolves with new competitors.
  • MoAs: PD-1 inhibitors (e.g., pembrolizumab) block the PD-1 receptor to enhance T-cell-mediated tumor killing; VEGF inhibitors (e.g., lenvatinib) block vascular endothelial growth factor to inhibit tumor angiogenesis.
  • Sources: BioSpace
